vocabulary.definition: shaky and not very strong or safe
vocabulary.example: There were a hundred and forty two staircases at Hogwarts: wide, sweeping ones; narrow, rickety ones; some that led somewhere different on a Friday;
some with a vanishing step halfway up that you had to remember to jump.
Filch owned a cat called Mrs. Norris, a scrawny, dust colored creature with bulging, lamp like eyes just like Filch’s.

vocabulary.definition: a stick used to perform magic
vocabulary.example: There was a lot more to magic, as Harry quickly found out, than waving your wand and saying a few funny words.
Strict and clever, she gave them a talking to the moment they sat down in her first class.

vocabulary.definition: a creature that drinks blood and fears garlic
vocabulary.example: His classroom smelled strongly of garlic, which everyone said was to ward off a vampire he’d met in Romania and was afraid would be coming back to get him one of these days.
“Ah, Yes,” he said softly, “Harry Potter. Our new—celebrity.”

vocabulary.definition: to cook liquids gently just below boiling
vocabulary.example: I don’t expect you will really understand the beauty of the softly simmering cauldron with its shimmering fumes, the delicate power of liquids that creep through human veins, bewitching the mind, ensnaring the senses…

vocabulary.definition: to smile in a mean, mocking way
vocabulary.example: Snape’s lips curled into a sneer.
I can teach you how to bottle fame, brew glory, even stopper death—if you aren’t as big a bunch of dunderheads as I usually have to teach.”
Neville had somehow managed to melt Seamus’s cauldron into a twisted blob, and their potion was seeping across the stone floor, burning holes in people’s shoes.
Hagrid had emptied vault seven hundred and thirteen, if you could call it emptying, taking out that grubby little package.

characters.name: Harry Potter
characters.description: Still famous at Hogwarts and struggling to find his way to classes.

characters.name: Professor Snape
characters.description: The cold Potions master who seems to hate Harry on sight.

characters.name: Ron Weasley
characters.description: Harry’s friend who shares his first miserable Potions lesson.

characters.name: Rubeus Hagrid
characters.description: Invites Harry for tea and seems uneasy about the Gringotts break-in.

characters.name: Argus Filch
characters.description: The caretaker who patrols corridors with his cat, Mrs. Norris.

background.hook: Harry’s first week at Hogwarts is confusing and crowded with whispers. His first Potions lesson with Professor Snape goes badly, but tea with Hagrid raises a troubling question about the Gringotts break-in.
background.readingFocus: Watch how Snape treats Harry compared to Malfoy, and notice what Hagrid won’t say about the newspaper clipping on his table.

options.text: lets students do whatever they want

comprehension.stem: What is this chapter mainly about?

options.text: Harry’s first week of classes, a harsh Potions lesson with Snape, and a revealing tea with Hagrid
Harry’s Sorting into Gryffindor at the welcome feast
Harry’s first flying lesson and Quidditch tryout
Harry shopping for supplies in Diagon Alley

comprehension.explanation: The chapter covers Hogwarts classes, Snape’s Potions lesson, and Harry’s visit to Hagrid’s hut.

comprehension.stem: How does Harry first get on Filch’s bad side?

options.text: He and Ron try to open a door that leads to the out-of-bounds third-floor corridor
He drops chalk on Filch’s head
He feeds Mrs. Norris rock cakes
He uses magic in the corridor between classes

comprehension.explanation: Filch catches them at the forbidden corridor door and refuses to believe they are lost.

comprehension.stem: What potion ingredient does Snape say comes from a goat’s stomach?
comprehension.explanation: Snape tells the class a bezoar is a stone from a goat’s stomach that saves you from most poisons.

comprehension.stem: How many Gryffindor points does Harry lose in his first Potions lesson?
comprehension.explanation: Snape takes one point for cheek and another because Harry didn’t stop Neville adding quills too soon.

comprehension.stem: How does Harry feel about Snape by the end of the first Potions lesson?

options.text: He realizes Snape hates him, not just dislikes him
He thinks Snape is his favorite teacher
He is sure Snape will recommend him for Quidditch
He believes Snape is afraid of him

comprehension.explanation: Harry had thought Snape disliked him; by the lesson’s end he knows Snape hates him.

comprehension.stem: Why does Harry think Hagrid is hiding something about the Gringotts break-in?

options.text: Hagrid won’t meet Harry’s eyes and changes the subject
Hagrid shows Harry the vault key
Hagrid calls the newspaper clipping a fake
Hagrid says Snape broke into Gringotts

comprehension.explanation: When Harry mentions the break-in on his birthday, Hagrid grunts and avoids his eyes.

comprehension.stem: What does Hagrid invite Harry to do on Friday afternoon?

options.text: Come for a cup of tea around three
Try out for the Quidditch team

comprehension.explanation: Hedwig delivers a note asking Harry to tea because he has Friday afternoons off.

vocabulary.explanation: Snape calls his students dunderheads unless they prove otherwise.
Snape sneers when Harry cannot answer his questions.

options.text: turn a desk into a pig

vocabulary.explanation: Neville’s ruined potion seeps across the dungeon floor.

vocabulary.stem: Which word is closest in meaning to 'grubby'?
vocabulary.explanation: Harry remembers Hagrid taking a grubby little package from vault 713.

vocabulary.stem: “Ah, Yes,” he said softly, “Harry Potter. Our new—___.”
vocabulary.explanation: Snape mocks Harry’s fame during roll call.

cloze.title: The Potions Master
paragraphs: Hogwarts was hard to navigate, with (1) ___ staircases and even a (2) ___ step. Filch’s (3) ___ cat, Mrs. Norris, patrolled the corridors.
In Potions, Snape called Harry a (4) ___ and curled his lips into a (5) ___. When Neville’s cauldron melted, potion began (6) ___ across the floor.
At tea, Harry read about the Gringotts break-in and wondered about the (7) ___ little package Hagrid had taken from vault 713.

gaps.explanation: Some Hogwarts staircases are narrow and rickety.
Some stairs have a vanishing step you must jump over.
Mrs. Norris is a scrawny, dust-colored cat.
Snape introduces Harry as their new celebrity.
Snape sneers when Harry cannot answer his questions.
The ruined potion seeps across the stone floor.
Harry recalls the grubby package from vault 713.
